profile.blocker: I’m trying to do too much
profile.coreProblem: You are trying to improve everything at once, which is killing momentum.
profile.identityShift: Start seeing yourself as someone who creates value daily instead of waiting for motivation to appear.
profile.fastestWin: Discipline is your force multiplier. Once that tightens up, every other area gets easier.
next72Hours: Today: Run one focused money move every day with a 30-minute+ daily reset: outreach, selling, pitching, or building one skill that compounds.
Tonight: Remove junk-food friction: pre-plan one easy meal, keep water visible, and stop relying on willpower when you are tired.
Within 72 hours: lock in one repeatable daily standard and track it with a visible checkmark.
weekOneChecklist: Protect a 30-minute+ performance block every day.
Pre-decide one default meal or snack so you stop improvising under stress.
Use one visible tracker and do not let two misses happen in a row.
profile.headline: Money track: 50/100
profile.summary: Your next breakthrough is going to come from removing friction and tightening your system, not trying harder.
plan: Run one focused money move every day with a 30-minute+ daily reset: outreach, selling, pitching, or building one skill that compounds.
Remove junk-food friction: pre-plan one easy meal, keep water visible, and stop relying on willpower when you are tired.
Use tonight as the line in the sand: clean your space, plan tomorrow, and make your standards obvious before motivation fades.
